NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Systems

Public Publishing Boundary

The site is a static publication layer, separate from source control, internal tools, and any future operating console.

The Northroot site is a static publication layer.

The first version intentionally excludes a CMS, production deploy automation, analytics, comments, sign-in, and hosted control-plane behavior. Local Markdown editing, local preview, and pull request review are enough.

source work -> generated draft -> human edit -> merge-gated publish

Implementation Notes

Generated drafts must carry source references, draft status, and a clear review path before they can become public pages.

Boundary Map

AreaRole
Public siteExplanations, docs, examples, journal notes.
GitHub repoSource of truth for code, specs, examples, and review.
DraftsEditorial inputs, never automatic publication.
Internal toolsOut of scope for the public site until real software exists.

Operational Lessons

The publication layer should explain the system. It should not become the system.

Source

northroot/site/README.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
